Text: I chose him due to his exemplary record as a Circuit Court of Appeals Judge, and I was very satisfied when a predominantly Democratic United States Senate, after scrutinizing his background, agreed he was suitable to serve, with an overwhelming vote supporting him.
examples: I selected him because of his outstanding record as a Circuit Court of Appeals Judge, and I was very pleased that uh - an overwhelming Democratic United States Senate, after going into his background, came to the conclusion that he was uh - fit and should serve, and the vote in his behalf was overwhelming.

Text: In my opinion, that's incorrect, and I believe we have the potential to improve.
examples: I think that's wrong, and I think we can do better.

Text: I'm interested in hearing solely about whether it's beneficial or detrimental for people—is it ethically justifiable?
examples: I want to hear only arguments as to whether it is good or bad for the people -- is it morally right?

Text: This isn't related to President Clinton.
examples: It's not about President Clinton.

Text: That action will, in fact, bring down expenses.
examples: That will -- that will reduce costs.

Text: What has occurred with you?
examples: What has happened to you?

Text: I believe you'll meet someone who could pique your interest.
examples: I think you'll find somebody who might interest you.

Text: Please refrain from alarming the elderly, Mr. President.
examples: Stop scaring the seniors, Mr. President.

Text: We might discover that later this evening.
examples: Perhaps we might find that out tonight.

Text: It seems to me that this Republican administration has largely been about appearances and flair without much substance.
examples: Well I think this Republican administration has been almost all style, and spectacular, and not substance.

Text: For small businesses, it's crucial to have capital, access to credit, and for many, guidance from mentors or coaches is necessary.
examples: Small business--you've got to have capital, you've got to credit, and many of them need mentors or coaches.

Text: Consequently, this represents a significant menace.
examples: So this is a serious threat.

Text: Lastly, my concluding remark is this: Mr. Ford and Mr. Kissinger have perpetuated Richard Nixon's strategies and shortcomings.
examples: And the last point I wanna make is this: Mr. Ford, Mr. Kissinger have uh - continued on with the policies and failures of Richard Nixon.

Text: I suppose it's due to the fact that you'll forfeit your ability to dictate their thoughts.
examples: I guess it's because you will lose your right to tell them what to think.

Text: It is imperative that we provide quality education to every citizen, rather than limiting it to the select few.
examples: We have to make quality education there for all of our citizens, not just the privileged few.

Text: An approach akin to Bowles-Simpson might be to examine each deduction individually, thus creating changes incrementally.
examples: One could follow Bowles-Simpson as a model and take deduction by deduction and make differences that way.

Text: If all else fails, resort to atomic energy.
examples: And then as a last resort only, continue to use atomic power.

Text: It's a stark difference, in my opinion.
examples: I think it's a very clear contrast.

Text: To assist in recruitment efforts.
examples: As an aid to enlistment.

Text: Assemble your strategies by Christmas, and be prepared to act when Congress does; finalize everything.
examples: Now, put together your plans by Christmas, be ready to go when Congress goes, nail these things.

Text: The President announced a reduction in the size of his administration.
examples: The President just said that his government is shrinking.

Text: A few had visited previously.
examples: Some of them had been there before.

Text: Maybe Mrs. Truman is able, but I doubt my own ability.
examples: Perhaps Mrs. Truman can, but I don't think I can.

Text: We're committed to enhancing adoption rates nationally.
examples: We'll do everything we can to improve adoption in this country.

Text: Washington is overwhelmed with resentment.
examples: There's too much bitterness in Washington.

Text: "We" refers to both of us.
examples: The "we" is you and me.

Text: Allow me to articulate one final remark.
examples: Well, let me just make a closing point.

Text: Regarding the war, you can't claim you sided with the minority yet would have likely voted with the majority.
examples: You can't do it on the war, where he says well, I was with the minority but I guess I would have voted with the majority.

Text: The strategic deployment of military forces can contribute to maintaining peace.
examples: A judicious use of the military which will help keep the peace.

Text: They attempted it with leaders like Franklin Roosevelt, Harry Truman, and John Kennedy.
examples: They tried it with Franklin Roosevelt and Harry Truman and John Kennedy.

Text: Such an event would significantly compromise our capabilities in the Pacific region.
examples: And that would be a severe blow to our abilities there in the Pacific.

Text: However, Mr. Vice President, I must tell you that we are satisfied with the legislation we currently have.
examples: But let me say to you, Mr. Vice President, we're happy with our laws on our books.

Text: I am firmly convinced we ought to act in Formosa and fulfill our obligations to all the nations for which we've pledged security.
examples: I believe strongly we should d- do so in Formosa and I believe we should meet our commitments to every country whose security we've guaranteed.

Text: The optional national assessments are on top of the compulsory evaluations we mandate for states.
examples: The voluntary national testing is in addition to the mandatory testing that we require of states.

Text: Is the individual accountable for the offense?
examples: Is the person guilty of the crime?

Text: Ultimately, outcomes are what truly matter.
examples: Now, results is what counts.

Text: No, it's more than just political talk.
examples: No, it's not just campaign rhetoric.

Text: Take NATO as an example; it's maintained peace for countless years. I wish for us to remain fully engaged with NATO, ensuring ongoing peace in Europe.
examples: NATO, for example, has kept the peace for many, many years, and I want to see us keep fully staffed in NATO so we'll continue to guarantee the peace in Europe.

Text: I recognize that some within our party hold opposing views on this matter.
examples: And I know there are some in our party that disagree with that sentiment.

Text: There is no intention on my part to reduce education funding or the grants allocated to college students.
examples: I don't have any plan to cut education funding and -- and grants that go to people going to college.

Text: Describing the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, who has served his country for thirty-five years, in such a manner seems inappropriate, and I'm confident the governor shares this perspective.
examples: I don't think that's a proper way to describe aayuh- chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff who has fought for his country for thirty-five years, and I'm sure the governor would agree with me on that.

Text: I acknowledge that prices might rise, because as you progress and need to delve deeper, it increases production costs.
examples: I grant you that prices may go up, because as you go further and have to go deeper, you are adding to the cost of production.

Text: However, turning this into a contentious political matter and lacking bipartisan backing poses a problem -- I can bring a significant number of Democrats to achieve comprehensive immigration reform, yet we can't...
examples: But when we make this into a divisive political issue, and when we don't have bipartisan support -- I can deliver, Governor, a whole bunch of Democrats to get comprehensive immigration reform done, and we can't...

Text: Opportunities will diminish; there will be increased deficits, more drug issues, higher crime rates, and diminished faith among the American populace.
examples: You're not going to have the opportunities; there are going to be more deficits, more drugs, more crime, and less confidence in the American people.

Text: Certainly, there remains further work to be done.
examples: Sure, there's more work to do.

Text: The falsehood about birth origins was deeply damaging.
examples: And the birther lie was a very hurtful one.

Text: The core of the issue is a choice between trust and fear, and honestly, Mr. President, fear seems to be your main strategy in this campaign.
examples: It's a question between trust and fear, and I would say, I think, Mr. President, about all you've got going in this campaign is fear.

Text: Certain members in our party hold a differing opinion on this matter.
examples: And I know there are some in our party that disagree with that sentiment.

Text: All of those could potentially be contested by men.
examples: Those would all, could all be challenged by men.

Text: [Laughter and applause] Should there be time left, Mr. Trewhitt, I might mention that either Seneca or Cicero—it escapes me which one—once remarked, "Without the elders amending the errors of youth, there would be no state."
examples: [Laughter and applause] If I still have time, I might add, Mr. Trewhitt, I might add that it was Seneca or it was Cicero, I don't know which, that said, "If it was not for the elders correcting the mistakes of the young, there would be no state.''
